question:
why was the man lying flat on his tummy nearing the end

Candidates:
A) sleeping
B) catch dog
C) check out the camera
D) fell from his board
E) to cushion the boy s fall

Answer the question and reference key frames with [FRAME:t=...] markers.
cot: At [FRAME:t=13.0], the man is seen lying on his back in the snow, limbs spread, indicating a fall. The snow-covered environment and his prone posture suggest he has just landed after falling. At [FRAME:t=14.0], he remains motionless in the same position, confirming he is not moving voluntarily. The presence of a board nearby, visible in earlier frames such as [FRAME:t=13.0], supports the context of a snowboarding or similar activity. His flat, supine position is inconsistent with sleeping, checking a camera, catching a dog, or cushioning a boy’s fall. The evidence strongly indicates he fell from his board.
